QF.id: XXVA Introduction to Research Analyst Framework_24

question.text: Which of the following best describes the relationship between SEBI and the stock exchanges it recognizes?

answer.text: SEBI owns and operates all stock exchanges in India
SEBI grants recognition to stock exchanges, supervises their operations, and has the power to make or amend their bye-laws
Stock exchanges are completely independent and SEBI has no supervisory authority over them
SEBI only conducts annual audits of stock exchanges without any other regulatory power

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SCRA 1956, SEBI grants recognition to stock exchanges, continuously supervises them, and has the power to make or amend their bye-laws. It can also supersede the governing body or suspend operations if necessary. **The Trap:** SEBI doesn't own exchanges — they are independent entities. But they are NOT beyond SEBI's regulatory authority — recognition, supervision, and bye-law powers ensure comprehensive oversight. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XII and VII cover exchange regulation. Series XV covers it in the broader regulatory infrastructure context. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Exchange circulars and surveillance mechanisms (ASM, GSM) are SEBI-mandated tools — understanding them helps interpret research recommendations and identify stocks under regulatory watch.
